title: Introduction to Java
theory_text: Java is an Object-Oriented Programming language. In this lesson, you will learn how to create your first class. A class is a blueprint for creating objects. Every Java program needs at least one class.

title: Variables and Data Types
theory_text: Variables are containers for storing data values. Java has different types: int for integers, double for decimals, String for text, and boolean for true/false. Always declare the type before the variable name.

title: Object-Oriented Programming
theory_text: OOP is a programming paradigm based on the concept of objects. Objects contain data (fields) and code (methods). The four pillars are: Encapsulation, Inheritance, Polymorphism, and Abstraction.
